rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) as an operational source for hedonic versus utilitarian classification, explicitly adopting their definitions in Study 1 and their scale in Study 3 pretests. This citation is used to set up design controls and rule out alternative explanations (for example, that effects are driven by hedonic indulgence rather than incommensurability). Although it is not the core theory being extended, it meaningfully informs measurement and interpretation in the experiments.
relation_description: The citing paper applies construct definitions and a measurement scale from Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) to classify products as hedonic or utilitarian in its studies. This borrowed operationalization is used to validate stimuli and support interpretation of the promotion effects.
